items.title: OpenAI Expands Codex Into Desktop Agent Platform (3 minute read)
items.titleZh: OpenAI 将 Codex 扩展为桌面代理平台（阅读时间3分钟）
items.summary:
# AI Tool Brief  
*Your weekly roundup of AI tool updates — April 17, 2026*  

---

OpenAI just revamped its Codex coding tool to fight back against Anthropic’s leading Claude Code. Here’s the breakdown:  

## Top Story: Codex Gets Agentic Superpowers  
OpenAI’s Codex now runs **background agents on Macs** that work parallel to your tasks (no interference!). Use cases: iterating on frontend changes, testing apps, or operating in non-API tools. The update mirrors Anthropic’s recent Claude & Cowork desktop control feature, as both battle for enterprise coding dominance.  

## Quick Bites  
- **In-app browser**: Command Codex to act on web apps (future: full browser control for dev/game work).  
- **Memory (preview)**: Recalls past sessions to adapt to your workflow.  
- **Image generation**: Creates product concepts, slides, or mockups.  
- **111 plugins**: Integrates with CodeRabbit/GitLab for clerical tasks (e.g., Slack/calendar to-do lists).  
- **Flexible pricing**: Pay-as-you-go for ChatGPT enterprise/business users.  

## Context  
OpenAI’s shift to enterprise tools (retreating from consumer Sora 2) comes amid competition with Anthropic and recent lawsuits over ChatGPT’s alleged mental health impacts.  

---  
*Next week: More AI tool launches. Subscribe for updates.*
items.summaryZh:
# AI工具简报  
每周AI工具更新汇总 | 2026年4月17日  

---

OpenAI近期更新了其Codex编码工具，以应对Anthropic领先的Claude Code。以下是详细情况：  

## 头条：Codex新增智能代理功能  
OpenAI的Codex现支持**Mac后台代理**，可与你的任务并行运行（互不干扰！）。适用场景：前端变更迭代、应用测试或非API工具操作。此次更新与Anthropic近期推出的Claude & Cowork桌面控制功能类似，双方正争夺企业编码领域的主导地位。  

## 快速看点  
- **应用内浏览器**：可指令Codex操作网页应用（未来将支持开发者/游戏工作所需的完整浏览器控制）。  
- **记忆功能（预览版）**：可回忆过往会话，适配你的工作流。  
- **图像生成**：可生成产品概念、幻灯片或原型图。  
- **111款插件**：与CodeRabbit、GitLab集成，可处理事务性任务（如Slack/日历待办清单）。  
- **灵活定价**：ChatGPT企业版/商业版用户可选择按使用量付费。  

## 背景  
OpenAI转向企业级工具（减少对消费级Sora 2的投入），这一变化源于与Anthropic的竞争，以及近期ChatGPT被指控影响心理健康的相关诉讼。  

---  
*下周预告：更多AI工具发布。订阅获取最新动态。*
items.content: –:–:–:– The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW. Close TechCrunch Desktop Logo TechCrunch Mobile Logo LatestStartupsVentureAppleSecurityAIApps EventsPodcastsNewsletters SearchSubmit Site Search Toggle Mega Menu Toggle Topics Latest AI Amazon Apps Biotech & Health Climate Cloud Computing Commerce Crypto Enterprise EVs Fintech Fundraising Gadgets Gaming Google Government & Policy Hardware Instagram Layoffs Media & Entertainment Meta Microsoft Privacy Robotics Security Social Space Startups TikTok Transportation Venture More from TechCrunch Staff Events Startup Battlefield StrictlyVC Newsletters Podcasts Videos Partner Content TechCrunch Brand Studio Crunchboard Contact Us Image Credits:OpenAI AI OpenAI takes aim at Anthropic with beefed-up Codex that gives it more power over your desktop Lucas Ropek 12:18 PM PDT · April 16, 2026 There is currently a low-grade war between OpenAI and Anthropic over who can release the most convenient and powerful AI-coding tools and, so far, Anthropic seems to be winning. Claude Code has been dubbed the tool of choice for many businesses, as TechCrunch reported last week, but OpenAI isn’t giving up yet. This week, OpenAI announced a revamp of Codex, its own automated tool, with a variety of new updates designed to give it significantly expanded powers. On Thursday, the company announced a plethora of new features and updates, perhaps the most notable of which is that Codex can now operate in the background on your computer — opening any app on your desktop and carrying out operations with a cursor that clicks and types. Functionally, what this does is allow Codex to deploy multiple agents, all of which work on a user’s Mac “in parallel, without interfering with your own work in other apps,” the company said in a blog post. In other words, because of the way Codex runs in the background, a user can still be using the machine as the agent goes about its own work. The agent will then function, according to the company, as a kind of coding buddy that does auxiliary tasks while you work on topline projects. OpenAI’s lists “iterating on frontend changes, testing apps, or working in apps that don’t expose an API” as potential use cases for this kind of agentic assistance. Overall, this agentic update and other new additions demonstrate OpenAI’s desire to not only make Codex a competitive coding assistant but also a more multifaceted tool that can be integrated into a variety of corporate workflows. Watchers of the AI coding space will also note that some of the powers OpenAI is now adding to Codex seem to resemble those previously released by Anthropic for Claude Code. Last month, Anthropic announced that Claude and Cowork could remotely control your Mac and desktop on a user’s behalf while they were away from their keyboard. In addition to the agentic tools, OpenAI’s Codex now has an in-app browser, which allows a user to issue commands to the agentic tool, which it will then ostensibly carry out on specific web applications. OpenAI says this function will be useful for front-end and game development, and that it plans to eventually expand the capability so that Codex can “fully command the browser beyond web applications on localhost.” Techcrunch event Meet your next investor or portfolio startup at Disrupt Your next round. Your next hire. Your next breakout opportunity. Find it at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, where 10,000+ founders, investors, and tech leaders gather for three days of 250+ tactical sessions, powerful introductions, and market-defining innovation. Register now to save up to $410. Meet your next investor or portfolio startup at Disrupt Your next round. Your next hire. Your next breakout opportunity. Find it at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, where 10,000+ founders, investors, and tech leaders gather for three days of 250+ tactical sessions, powerful introductions, and market-defining innovation. Register now to save up to $410. San Francisco, CA | October 13-15, 2026 REGISTER NOW There are other updates. A new feature in preview called “memory” allows Codex to recall previous work sessions and generate important context about how a particular user works. The agent has also been given a new image-generation ability, which OpenAI says can be used to create product concepts, slide visuals, mockups, placeholder images, and other corporate paraphernalia. Finally, to expand Codex’s ability to get things done, the company has announced 111 plug-in integrations from apps like CodeRabbit and GitLab Issues, which allows Codex to carry out tasks involving those tools. The way OpenAI has framed it, these plug-ins give Codex the ability to carry out minor clerical work to organize your work life. For example, if you want Codex to take a look at your Slack channels and Google calendar and give you a to-do list for a given day, OpenAI says that it can now do that for you. A new pay-as-you-go Codex pricing option for ChatGPT enterprise and business customers has also been announced in an apparent effort to give users more flexibility when it comes to procuring the coding tool’s services. Once considered the undisputed leader of its industry, OpenAI has more fiercely competed with Anthropic in recent months, with a focus on enterprise capabilities and a retreat from consumer tools like its social video app Sora 2. The company has also battled various controversies in recent months, including lawsuits over ChatGPT’s alleged mental health impact on some users. Topics agents, AI, Anthropic, claude code, codex, Enterprise, OpenAI Lucas Ropek Senior Writer, TechCrunch Lucas is a senior writer at TechCrunch, where he covers artificial intelligence, consumer tech, and startups. He previously covered AI and cybersecurity at Gizmodo. You can contact Lucas by emailing lucas.ropek@techcrunch.com. View Bio April 30 San Francisco, CA StrictlyVC kicks off the year in SF. items.title: Salesforce Pushes Agent-First Enterprise With Headless 360 (6 minute read)
items.titleZh: Salesforce 推出Headless 360，推动座席优先企业战略（阅读时长6分钟）
items.summary:
# Enterprise Tech Brief  
*Critical enterprise tech updates — Apr 16, 2026*  

---

## Top Story: Salesforce’s Headless 360 for Agent-First Workflows  
Salesforce launched **Headless 360**, an API-driven platform packaging its AI/developer tools (including Agentforce Vibes) to enable agent-first enterprise processes. Agents directly access Salesforce’s Data 360/Customer 360 stacks (APIs, workflows, governance) without separate integrations—cutting reliance on human interfaces.  

## Analyst Insights  
- **Futurum’s Dion Hinchcliffe**: Shift from "system of record" to cross-tool "system of execution" for agents.  
- **Info-Tech’s Scott Bickley**: Warnings: No cost/licensing details (risk of premium tiers), immature governance tools (need 12-18 months of enterprise supplementation), and vendor lock-in risks (modern data stacks offer more flexibility).  
- **Dev wins**: Full access to external coding agents (Claude Code, Codex) and AI-powered DevOps Center MCP to fix fragile Salesforce pipelines.  

## Rollout Notes  
GA now: Agentforce Vibes 2.0, DevOps Center MCP, Session Tracing, Agentforce Experience Layer. Early access: Custom Scoring Evals. May/June: Testing Center, Salesforce Catalog.  

## Closing  
CIOs should push for extended pilots/sandboxes to validate governance maturity and avoid hidden costs before adopting Headless 360.  

---  
*Subscribe for weekly enterprise tech deep dives*
items.summaryZh:
# 企业技术简报  
*企业技术重要更新 — 2026年4月16日*  

---

## 头条：Salesforce推出面向坐席优先工作流的Headless 360  
Salesforce推出Headless 360，这是一款API驱动的平台，整合了其AI及开发者工具（包括Agentforce Vibes），支持坐席优先的企业流程。坐席无需额外集成即可直接访问Salesforce的Data 360/Customer 360套件（API、工作流、治理功能），减少对人工界面的依赖。  

## 分析师观点  
- **Futurum分析师Dion Hinchcliffe**：坐席端将从「记录系统」转向跨工具的「执行系统」。  
- **Info-Tech分析师Scott Bickley**：风险提示：无成本/许可细节（存在高级套餐风险）、治理工具不成熟（需12-18个月企业补充）、供应商锁定风险（现代数据套件灵活性更高）。  
- **开发者利好**：可完全访问外部编码助手（Claude Code、Codex），以及AI驱动的DevOps Center MCP，用于修复脆弱的Salesforce流水线。  

## 发布说明  
正式可用（GA）：Agentforce Vibes 2.0、DevOps Center MCP、会话追踪、Agentforce体验层。  
抢先体验：自定义评分评估。  
5-6月：测试中心、Salesforce目录。  

## 结语  
CIO应推动延长试点/沙盒环境使用，验证治理成熟度并避免隐性成本后，再决定是否采用Headless 360。  

---  
*订阅获取每周企业技术深度解析*
items.content: TopicsNewsOpinionNewslettersResourcesBuyer's GuidesEvents Editions Search Menu Topics Close AnalyticsArtificial IntelligenceBusiness OperationsCareersCloud ComputingData CenterData ManagementDigital TransformationDiversity and InclusionEmerging TechnologyEnterprise ApplicationsEnterprise Buyer’s GuidesGenerative AIIndustryInnovationIT LeadershipIT ManagementIT OperationsIT StrategyNetworkingProject ManagementSecuritySoftware DevelopmentVendors and Providers Back Close Search US-EN TopicsNewsOpinionNewslettersResourcesBuyer's GuidesEventsMoreAwardsBlogsBrandHubsBrandPostsCXO OutlookDEMOBuyer’s GuidesEventsPodcastsVideos Topics TopicsAnalyticsArtificial IntelligenceBusiness OperationsCareersCloud ComputingData CenterData ManagementDigital TransformationDiversity and InclusionEmerging TechnologyEnterprise ApplicationsEnterprise Buyer’s GuidesGenerative AIIndustryInnovationIT LeadershipIT ManagementIT OperationsIT StrategyNetworkingProject ManagementSecuritySoftware DevelopmentVendors and Providers AfricaAfricaAmericasCanadaUnited StatesAsiaASEANIndiaMiddle East日本 (Japan)대한민국(Korea)EuropeDeutschland (Germany)España (Spain)IrelandItalia (Italy)NetherlandsUnited KingdomOceaniaAustraliaNew Zealand Home Artificial Intelligence by Anirban Ghoshal Senior Writer Salesforce launches Headless 360 to support agent-first enterprise workflows News Apr 16, 20266 mins The new platform packages Salesforce’s AI and developer tools into a headless, API‑driven layer designed for software agents to execute business processes without human interfaces. Credit: Tada Images / Shutterstock Salesforce is packaging its developer and AI tooling, including its vibe coding environment Agentforce Vibes, into a new platform named Headless 360, designed to help enterprise teams build agent-first workflows. The CRM software provider defines agent-first workflows as enterprise processes in which software agents, rather than human users, carry out tasks by directly invoking APIs, tools, and predefined business logic. To support this approach, Headless 360 exposes Salesforce’s underlying data, workflows, and governance controls as APIs, MCP tools, and CLI commands, via its existing offerings, such as Data 360, Customer 360, and Agentforce, Joe Inzerillo, president of AI technology at Salesforce, said during a press briefing. This allows agents to operate directly on the platform’s existing business logic and datasets, rather than relying on separate integrations or user interfaces, Inzerillo added. Push to become a control layer for enterprise AI agents Analysts, however, see Headless 360 as an effort by Salesforce to position itself as a central layer for managing agent-driven operations across different business functions in enterprises, moving from a system of record to being the system of execution. “Salesforce knows the center of gravity is moving toward coding agents, conversational interfaces, agent harnesses, and external runtimes, so it is trying to keep Salesforce relevant as the system underneath,” said Dion Hinchcliffe, VP of the CIO practice at The Futurum Group. With Headless 360, Hinchcliffe added, Salesforce is trying to move its positioning beyond “AI agents inside Salesforce” to framing “Salesforce as a programmable platform for agents operating across external tools, interfaces, and environments.” Analysts warn that CIOs need caution before adopting Headless 360. Scott Bickley, advisory fellow at Info-Tech Research Group, said modern data stacks can replicate much of Headless 360’s functionality with more flexibility and less vendor concentration. There are other issues that Bickley thinks should worry CIOs: “There is no mention of cost or the underlying licensing model for this ‘headless’ experience. Are all tools included at no cost?” “Salesforce’s MO seems to be to announce new capabilities that require SKUs. CIOs should be asking about pricing now, before building in architectural dependencies on features that might land in a premium cost tier,” Bickley cautioned. Also, the analyst pointed out that Salesforce’s announcement is silent on SLAs for operations such as MCP tool calls, which matter materially for real-time agent workflows. Incremental gains for developers despite broader concerns Despite these concerns, Bickley sees some of the new Headless 360 features, although undifferentiated from the competition, as offering practical benefits for developers in their daily tasks. The analyst was referring to newer updates, such as new MCP tools that give external coding agents full access to Salesforce’s platform, the DevOps Center MCP, the Agentforce Experience Layer, and newer governance features. Enabling full access to external coding agents, such as Claude Code and Codex, in particular, Bickley said, helps Salesforce to meet the developer where they are or let them continue using the tool of their choice. “Historically, developers were forced into Salesforce’s proprietary toolchain that included clunky VS Code extensions, painful metadata APIs, and quirky development pipelines that required Salesforce-specific expertise. Expanding the dev environment helps alleviate this pain,” Bickley pointed out. The other updates, according to Hinchcliffe, should help curtail developer friction by helping avoid frequent switching between development tools, expanding real-time awareness of organization data, reducing the need for custom plumbing to expose business logic, and decreasing the effort needed to move from prototype to deployment. Focusing specifically on the new DevOps Center MCP, which is a set of AI-powered tools that enable the use of natural language across the entire DevOps lifecycle, Bickley said that it will help developers alleviate pains around CI/CD processes. “Salesforce development pipelines are notoriously fragile with metadata dependencies, org-specific configurations, artificial limits on work items, and UI response issues, among others,” Bickley added. Concerns around the maturity of governance capabilities The governance tools, specifically the updates to the Testing Center, Custom Scoring Evals, Session Tracing, and A/B Testing API, according to Hinchcliffe, too, address real gaps that enterprise development teams face, especially moving agentic workflows or applications into production. “Salesforce is correctly identifying that enterprise agent adoption will stall unless buyers can properly measure, govern, debug, and tune agent behavior over time,” the analyst said. However, Bickley cautioned about the efficacy of these tools, as most of these tools are in the very early stages of their release. In fact, the analyst suggested that enterprises should expect to supplement these tools with their own evaluation frameworks for the next 12-18 months. The analyst also flagged additional concerns around newer components such as the Agentforce Experience Layer, which is a new UI service that allows developers to decouple what an agent does from how it surfaces across various services and applications. “Ironically, this adds yet another layer to contend with in the development process for what is already considered a painful development experience. Salesforce has a pattern of shipping v1 tools that work great in demos but fall in real-world scenarios,” Bickley said. “Development teams intending to avail themselves of these new feature sets should insist that Salesforce provide them an extended pilot and sandbox free of charge to validate the maturity level and ease of use of these new features,” Bickley added. All the updates to Headless 360, Salesforce said, are expected to be released in phases. Generally available features include Agentforce Vibes 2.0, the DevOps Center MCP, Session Tracing, and the Agentforce Experience Layer. Features that are in early access include Custom Scoring Evals. Other features, such as the Testing Center and the Salesforce Catalog, are scheduled for rollout in May and June, respectively. This story first appeared on InfoWorld. items.title: Agentic AI Is Failing on Orchestration, Not Tech (4 minute read)
items.titleZh: 智能体AI在编排而非技术上存在不足（阅读时长4分钟）
items.summary:
# TechForward Brief: Agentic AI Stalls (But It’s Not the Tech)  
*Your weekly enterprise tech insights — April 16, 2026*  

---

Agentic AI’s biggest roadblock isn’t tools or funding—it’s how businesses execute, per Qlik experts at their 2026 conference.  

## Why Initiatives Are Stuck  
Most enterprise agentic AI projects stall, and it’s not due to the tech stack. Randy Mickey (Qlik SVP of Global Professional Services) says the issue is **missing cross-department orchestration**: organizations fail to tie AI workflows directly to business outcomes, KPIs, and structured internal processes.  

## The Business-First Fix  
Qlik’s Natalie Rhoades (Global Senior Advisory Director) emphasizes starting with customer goals: “Understand strategic objectives and pain points first, then back into tech.” The smart scaling approach? **Build on existing systems** (no rip-and-replace) — Qlik’s modular platform supports this, even for customers with 20+ years of legacy investments.  

## Quick Actionable Takeaways  
For agentic AI success:  
✅ Align every AI workflow with measurable business outcomes  
✅ Coordinate across teams (not just silos)  
✅ Leverage existing tools (no need to start from scratch)  

---

Subscribe for weekly curated enterprise tech insights → [Link Placeholder]
items.summaryZh:
# TechForward简报：智能体AI遇阻（非技术之过）  
*每周企业科技洞察 — 2026年4月16日*  

Qlik专家在2026年大会上指出，智能体AI的最大障碍并非工具或资金，而是企业的落地执行能力。  

## 项目遇阻原因  
多数企业智能体AI项目陷入停滞，但并非技术栈问题。Qlik全球专业服务高级副总裁兰迪·米奇（Randy Mickey）表示，核心问题是**跨部门协同缺失**：企业未能将AI工作流直接关联业务成果、关键绩效指标（KPI）及结构化内部流程。  

## 以业务为核心的解决方案  
Qlik全球高级咨询总监娜塔莉·罗兹（Natalie Rhoades）强调，应从客户目标出发：“先明确战略目标与痛点，再反推技术选型。” 明智的规模化路径是？**基于现有系统迭代（而非推倒重来）** — Qlik的模块化平台支持这一方式，即便客户有20余年遗留系统投资也能适配。  

## 快速行动要点  
要实现智能体AI成功，需做到：  
✅ 每个AI工作流都关联可衡量的业务成果  
✅ 跨团队协同（打破部门壁垒）  
✅ 复用现有工具（无需从零开始）  

---

items.content: [the voice of enterprise and emerging tech] search OUR NETWORK menu account_circle search Home TechForward Awards Cloud Cloud Native AI Security Infra Blockchain Big Data Emerging Tech Policy Apps Iot Women In Tech theCUBE Research Special Reports Video Exclusives CUBE SPECIAL FEATURES RECENT CUBE EVENTS AWARDS SHARE Coverage from SiliconANGLE's livestreaming video studiohelp_outline UPDATED 12:48 EDT / APRIL 16 2026 AI Most agentic AI initiatives are stalling, and the reason has nothing to do with the tech stack by Ryan Stevens SHARE Agentic AI orchestration has become the engine of enterprise AI success, but technology alone won’t get it there. The real differentiator is business-led execution that ties AI workflows directly to outcomes, building on existing systems rather than replacing them. Agentic AI demands coordination across business departments and units. But many failures come from focusing too much on the technology instead of tying it back to business outcomes, KPIs and the structured path across the organization, according to Randy Mickey (pictured, left), senior vice president of global professional services, Qlik Technologies Inc. “What feeds what to whom, how, when, where — it’s just as important with agentic AI as when you go back to [enterprise resource planning], right? The whole thing with systems and business and outcomes, it’s a different flavor of that now,” Mickey said. “But when we talk to people that say, ‘Well, geez, I just can’t get this off the ground.’ It’s not the funding, it’s not the technology, it’s that orchestration that’s missing.” Mickey and Natalie Rhoades (right), global senior director for advisory services at Qlik, spoke with theCUBE’s Rob Strechay and Rebecca Knight at Qlik Connect 2026, during an exclusive broadcast on theCUBE, SiliconANGLE Media’s livestreaming studio. They discussed cross-department agentic AI orchestration and the importance of focusing on business outcomes and KPIs over a tech-first approach. (* Disclosure below.) Agentic AI orchestration from business goals Getting the technology right starts long before any tool is selected or any agent is deployed. From an advisory services perspective, the goal is to understand what the customer is trying to achieve, according to Rhoades. That means getting clear on their strategic objectives, pain points and goals. “Then we can back into the technology and determine what’s going to make the most sense for them,” Rhoades said. “We have an entire platform. It’s modular. We can pick and choose what we need to use from a Qlik perspective to support our customers. But it’s about ensuring that we get the right use case at the right time and we prioritize that.” The principle extends to scaling, according to Mickey. Organizations don’t need to scrap existing investments to move forward with agentic AI. Instead, the smarter path is to build on what’s already there, modernizing without ripping and replacing. “You’re not scrapping anything here. We’re building on top, we’re moving this to the next level,” Mickey said. “[Qlik CEO Mike Capone] talked today about all the Qlik customers that have potentially 20, 30 years worth of investment — we showed on stage how you can move that to an agentic experience.” Here’s the complete video interview, part of SiliconANGLE’s and theCUBE’s coverage of Qlik Connect 2026: (* Disclosure: TheCUBE is a paid media partner for Qlik Connect. Neither Qlik, the sponsor of theCUBE’s event coverage, nor other sponsors have editorial control over content on theCUBE or SiliconANGLE.) As the parent company of SiliconANGLE, theCUBE Network, theCUBE Research, CUBE365, theCUBE AI and theCUBE SuperStudios — with flagship locations in Silicon Valley and the New York Stock Exchange — SiliconANGLE Media operates at the intersection of media, technology and AI. items.summary:
# Azure App Modernization Playbook  
*Microsoft’s guide to turning app chaos into actionable transformation plans — [Current Date]*  

---

The biggest hurdle in app modernization isn’t moving to the cloud—it’s deciding which apps to prioritize, what to do with them, and how to allocate resources wisely. Microsoft Azure’s **App Modernization Playbook** solves this with a structured approach to app portfolio management.  

Key takeaways:  
- **Assess first**: Map your app portfolio to understand each app’s value, complexity, and growth opportunity.  
- **Choose action**: Decide to modernize, replatform, refactor, or leave apps as-is using real business signals (not guesswork).  
- **Architect smartly**: Match apps to the right Azure services without overengineering solutions.  
- **Execute clearly**: Translate strategy into concrete steps with clear tradeoffs and outcome alignment.  
- **Automate to accelerate**: Use intelligent agents to streamline discovery, assessments, and migration tasks.  

Speakers include Gayla Sheppard (Azure Data CVP) and John Macintyre (Azure Analytics Product Director).  

**Get the e-book**: Complete a short form to access the full guide.
items.summaryZh:
# Azure应用现代化指南  
微软指南：将应用混乱转化为可执行的转型计划 — [当前日期]  

---

应用现代化的最大障碍并非迁移到云，而是确定优先处理哪些应用、如何处理它们，以及如何合理分配资源。微软Azure的**应用现代化指南**通过结构化的应用组合管理方法解决了这一问题。  

核心要点：  
- **先评估**：梳理应用组合，了解每个应用的价值、复杂度及增长潜力。  
- **选择行动**：基于真实业务信号（而非猜测），决定是现代化、重新平台化、重构还是保持应用原样。  
- **明智架构**：为应用匹配合适的Azure服务，避免过度设计解决方案。  
- **清晰执行**：将战略转化为具体步骤，明确权衡利弊并对齐成果。  
- **自动化加速**：利用智能代理简化发现、评估及迁移任务。  

演讲嘉宾包括Gayla Sheppard（Azure数据事业部公司副总裁）和John Macintyre（Azure分析产品总监）。  

**获取电子书**：填写简短表单即可访问完整指南。

items.title: AI Agents Create New Observability Problem (3 minute read)
items.titleZh: AI智能体引发新的可观测性问题（阅读需3分钟）
items.summary:
# AI Enterprise Brief  
*Your weekly roundup of AI enterprise news and insights — April 17, 2026*  

---  

**Top Story: InsightFinder Raises $15M for AI Observability**  
InsightFinder AI closed a $15M Series B round led by Yu Galaxy this week. Founded by Helen Gu (NC State CS prof, ex-IBM/Google), the startup solves a critical gap: AI observability that spans data, models, and infrastructure (not just dev/test).  

**Quick Bite: Beyond LLM Checks**  
Unlike competitors (Grafana, Datadog), InsightFinder’s Autonomous Reliability Insights uses unsupervised ML, LLMs, and causal inference to find root causes. For example, it fixed a credit card client’s fraud model drift by identifying outdated server cache—*not* a model issue.  

**Customer Traction**  
Clients include UBS, NBCUniversal, Lenovo, Dell, Google Cloud, and Comcast. Revenue tripled in the past year, and investors approached the company after a 7-figure Fortune 50 deal (they weren’t actively fundraising).  

**What’s Next?**  
The $15M will fund first sales/marketing hires, expand their <30-person team, and boost go-to-market efforts. Total funding to date: $35M.  

---  
*Got an AI enterprise story? Reply to share!*
items.summaryZh:
# AI企业简报  
每周AI企业新闻与洞察汇总 — 2026年4月17日  

---  

**头条：InsightFinder获1500万美元融资 聚焦AI可观测性**  
InsightFinder AI本周完成1500万美元B轮融资，由宇星系（Yu Galaxy）领投。该初创公司由海伦·顾（Helen Gu，北卡罗来纳州立大学计算机系教授、前IBM/谷歌员工）创立，填补了一项关键空白：覆盖数据、模型及基础设施的AI可观测性（而非仅局限于开发/测试阶段）。  

**简讯：不止于大语言模型检测**  
与竞争对手（Grafana、Datadog）不同，InsightFinder的自主可靠性洞察平台采用无监督机器学习、大语言模型及因果推理定位根本原因。例如，它曾帮某信用卡客户解决欺诈模型漂移问题——关键在于识别出过时服务器缓存，而非模型本身故障。  

**客户进展**  
客户涵盖瑞银集团（UBS）、NBC环球、联想、戴尔、谷歌云及康卡斯特。过去一年营收翻三倍，且在拿下一笔七位数《财富》500强订单后，投资者主动接洽（当时公司并未积极融资）。  

**下一步计划**  
1500万美元将用于首次招聘销售/营销人员、扩充不足30人的团队，并加强市场推广。截至目前总融资额达3500万美元。  

---  
*有AI企业相关新闻？欢迎回复分享！*
items.content: –:–:–:– The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30. Tickets are going fast. Register now. Save up to $680 on your Disrupt 2026 pass. Ends 11:59 p.m. PT tonight. REGISTER NOW. Close TechCrunch Desktop Logo TechCrunch Mobile Logo LatestStartupsVentureAppleSecurityAIApps EventsPodcastsNewsletters SearchSubmit Site Search Toggle Mega Menu Toggle Topics Latest AI Amazon Apps Biotech & Health Climate Cloud Computing Commerce Crypto Enterprise EVs Fintech Fundraising Gadgets Gaming Google Government & Policy Hardware Instagram Layoffs Media & Entertainment Meta Microsoft Privacy Robotics Security Social Space Startups TikTok Transportation Venture More from TechCrunch Staff Events Startup Battlefield StrictlyVC Newsletters Podcasts Videos Partner Content TechCrunch Brand Studio Crunchboard Contact Us Image Credits:Aleksandr Pobeda / EyeEm (opens in a new window) / Getty Images Enterprise InsightFinder raises $15M to help companies figure out where AI agents go wrong Ram Iyer 9:15 AM PDT · April 16, 2026 The role of observability tools has evolved once again. While the market for solutions to ensure tech systems’ reliability has grown over the years, the center of gravity has steadily shifted from “track everything” to “control complexity and costs.” Meanwhile, the rapid influx and adoption of AI agents within enterprises have only added a brand-new category of workload that needs to be observed. InsightFinder AI, a startup based on 15 years of academic research, is no stranger to this problem. The company has been using machine learning to monitor, identify, and proactively fix IT infrastructure issues since 2016 and is now attacking today’s AI model reliability issue with an AI agent solution that can do everything from detection and diagnosis to remediation and prevention. The company, founded by CEO Helen Gu, a computer science professor at North Carolina State University who previously worked at IBM and Google, recently raised $15 million in a Series B round led by Yu Galaxy, TechCrunch has exclusively learned. According to Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong; it’s diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now that AI is a part of it. “In order to diagnose these AI model problems, you need to actually monitor and analyze the data, the model, and the infrastructure together,” Gu told TechCrunch. “It’s not always a model problem or a data problem; it’s a combination. Sometimes, it’s simply your infrastructure.” Gu explained how that looks in real life with an anecdote: One of its customers, a major U.S. credit card company, saw that one of its fraud-detection models was drifting. Because InsightFinder was monitoring all of the company’s infrastructure, it was able to identify that the model drift was caused by outdated cache in some server nodes. Find it at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, where 10,000+ founders, investors, and tech leaders gather for three days of 250+ tactical sessions, powerful introductions, and market-defining innovation. Register now to save up to $410. San Francisco, CA | October 13-15, 2026 REGISTER NOW “The biggest misconception is that AI observability is limited to LLM evaluation during the development and testing phases. On the contrary, a sound AI observability platform should provide end-to-end feedback loop support covering the development, evaluation, and production stages,” she said. InsightFinder’s newest product, dubbed Autonomous Reliability Insights, can do all this by using a combination of unsupervised machine learning, proprietary large and small language models, predictive AI, and causal inference. This base layer is data agnostic, per Gu, which lets the system ingest and analyze entire data streams to gather signals that can then be correlated and cross-validated to arrive at a root cause. Now, the observability space is crowded with contenders for a share of the new market that’s been opened up by the influx of AI tools. Nearly a decade into its journey, InsightFinder has been going up against the likes of Grafana Labs, Fiddler, Datadog, Dynatrace, New Relic, and BigPanda, who are all building capabilities to deal with the new problems presented by AI tools. But Gu isn’t fazed. On the contrary, she claims that InsightFinder’s expertise, experience, and customizability act as a sufficient moat. “We actually rarely lose [customers] to anybody so far … This is about the insights, right? The problem is that a lot of data scientists understand AI, but they don’t understand the system. And a lot of SRE [site reliability engineering] developers understand the system, but not the AI … They don’t look at it, and they don’t understand the intrinsic relationships.” Today, InsightFinder’s roster of customers includes UBS, NBCUniversal, Lenovo, Dell, Google Cloud, and Comcast. Gu attributes the success to 10 years of working to understand what large enterprise customers need. “It has come down to working with our Fortune 50 customers to polish and understand the enterprise environment requirements to deploy these kinds of models,” she said. “We have been working with Dell to deploy our AI systems across the world at some of the largest customers we have. This is not something that you can take a foundational AI and just slap on the machine data to do.” Gu said the company’s revenue stream is “strong,” having grown “over threefold” in the past year. In fact, she says InsightFinder wasn’t looking to raise this Series B at all, and investors approached the company after it won a seven-figure deal with a Fortune 50 company within three months. InsightFinder will use the fresh capital to make its first sales and marketing hires to expand its team of fewer than 30 people and invest in its go-to-market motion. The company has so far raised a total of $35 million. Topics AI, data observability, Datadog, Dynatrace, Enterprise, Fundraising, Grafana Labs, InsightFinder Ram Iyer Editor Ram is a financial and tech reporter and editor. items.title: Salesforce Expands AI Delivery with Elite Engineering Partner Network (6 minute read)
items.titleZh: Salesforce 携手精英工程合作伙伴网络 扩展AI交付服务（6分钟阅读）
items.summary:
# Salesforce Tech Brief  
*Salesforce product & partner updates — April 15, 2026*  

---

Salesforce launched its **Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) Partner Network** to scale Agentforce success, addressing a critical AI execution gap for enterprises.  

### Top Story: FDE Partner Network Launches  
30+ vetted partners (Accenture, Deloitte, PwC, IBM, Slalom, etc.) join the network, gaining direct access to Salesforce product teams, specialized training, and incentives tied to production outcomes (not just pilots).  

### Why It Matters  
IDC reports 1/3 of organizations are stuck in AI experimentation—few can scale pilots to enterprise-grade production. The FDE network bridges this gap with:  
- Compliance-aligned solution architecture (no retrofits)  
- Agentic design with built-in trust guardrails  
- Post-launch hypercare & change management  

### Customer Benefits  
- Faster pilot-to-production time  
- Industry-specific expertise (finance, healthcare, manufacturing)  
- Enterprise-grade compliance & security  

### Quick Take  
As Agentforce becomes Salesforce’s fastest-growing product, the FDE network turns AI ambition into measurable results. Find partners on AgentExchange to build your agentic workforce.  

---  
*Subscribe for weekly Salesforce insights*
items.summaryZh:
# Salesforce技术简报  
*Salesforce产品及合作伙伴更新 — 2026年4月15日*  

Salesforce正式推出**现场部署工程（FDE）合作伙伴网络**，旨在扩大Agentforce的成功应用，解决企业在AI落地执行中面临的关键缺口。  

### 重点新闻：FDE合作伙伴网络上线  
30余家经审核的合作伙伴（埃森哲、德勤、普华永道、IBM、Slalom等）已加入该网络，可直接对接Salesforce产品团队、获得专项培训，以及与实际投产成果挂钩的激励（而非仅试点项目）。  

### 核心价值  
IDC数据显示，1/3的企业陷入AI实验阶段——鲜少能将试点项目扩展至企业级生产环境。FDE网络通过以下方式填补这一缺口：  
- 符合合规要求的解决方案架构（无需后期改造）  
- 内置信任护栏的智能体设计  
- 上线后的深度支持及变更管理  

### 客户收益  
- 试点到投产周期缩短  
- 行业专属专业知识（金融、医疗、制造领域）  
- 企业级合规与安全  

### 速览  
随着Agentforce成为Salesforce增长最快的产品，FDE网络将企业的AI愿景转化为可衡量的成果。可在AgentExchange平台上寻找合作伙伴，打造您的智能体驱动工作团队。  

---  
*订阅获取每周Salesforce行业洞察*
items.content: Skip to Content Skip to Footer 0% Agents Salesforce Launches the Forward Deployed Engineering Partner Network to Scale Agentforce Success April 15, 2026 6 min read Salesforce is extending its engineering expertise and product roadmap insights to a select global partner network, significantly expanding access to trusted Agentforce experts worldwide. Launch partners Accenture and Deloitte are joined by over 30 firms, including PwC, Slalom, and IBM Consulting, all selected for their proven success with Agentforce. Salesforce’s FDE Partner Network combines the deep industry expertise with specialized Salesforce training. These partners will help drive Agentforce implementations at scale. Bridging the Gap Between Ambition and Results For the C-suite, the period of AI experimentation has ended and the mandate has shifted to delivering tangible business outcomes. However, a significant execution gap remains. IDC FutureScape* reports “over one-third of organizations will be stuck in the experimental, point solution phase of AI experimentation, requiring a shift of focus to enterprise use cases to deliver ROI.” Most businesses can build an initial proof of concept, but few have the technical foundation required to scale those pilots into systems that the business can actually run on. Becoming an Agentic Enterprise requires more than deploying models. It demands systems that can reason over proprietary data, operate within complex security environments, and deliver consistent, trusted outcomes. Salesforce is meeting this demand by expanding customer access to specialized Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) expertise, providing businesses with more ways to bridge the gap between AI ambition and real-world results. Scaling Elite Engineering to Meet Global Demand Salesforce has long supported customers through a trusted portfolio of success offerings, including Professional Services, implementation partners, and Success Plans. With Agentforce emerging as the fastest-growing product in company history, Salesforce is increasing its investment in these offerings to support tens of thousands of customers worldwide. The launch of the Salesforce FDE Partner Network provides businesses with the engineering depth required to achieve measurable outcomes at scale. By extending internal learnings and specialized training to a select group of partners, Salesforce helps to ensure that customers can turn unique business IP into a production-ready reality. “Our partner ecosystem is a massive competitive advantage, and the Salesforce FDE Partner Network operationalizes that advantage for the agentic era. The most successful organizations don’t just invest in Agentforce. They align with partners who possess the engineering and industry knowledge depth to turn that technology into the engine that transforms them into Agentic Enterprises.”— Miguel Milano, President & Chief Revenue Officer, Salesforce The network provides partners with a direct link to internal product teams, offering expert backing at the pace of Salesforce innovation to de-risk deployments and solve complex data challenges. By tying partner incentives to agents reaching production, Salesforce also helps to ensure that every engagement delivers measurable business value. “Getting AI into production is an engineering discipline. We know this because we’ve done it, and at enterprise scale. The Salesforce FDE Partner Network takes what we’ve built internally — the skills, capabilities, and high standards — and expands the talent and partnership with our elite network of partners. For customers, that means faster time to value and the absolute confidence of an enterprise-tested deployment.” — Lori Steele, President, Global Professional Services, Salesforce Proven Expertise, Engineered for Success The Salesforce FDE Partner Network is an exclusive designation that prioritizes technical rigor and production outcomes over firm size or tenure. Every member is a vetted partner with a proven track record of delivering Agentforce; to date, firms in the FDE Partner Network have driven one-third of all successful Agentforce implementations. By merging this technical proficiency with decades of knowledge in industries like financial services, healthcare, and manufacturing, these firms ensure that an enterprise-grade digital workforce is engineered with the industry expertise required to succeed in complex regulatory environments. Launch partners Accenture and Deloitte are joined by a global network of leaders including Capgemini, Cognizant, IBM Consulting, KPMG, PwC, Slalom, and Tata Consultancy Services, alongside regional experts such as InspireAI, OSF Digital, Rosetree Solutions, TerraSky, Twoday Oy and more than 20 additional partner firms. This global reach enables customers to have access to engineering expertise regardless of location or operational model, facilitating a secure and compliant path to building a digital workforce tailored to specific industry requirements. “The Salesforce FDE Partner Network is a critical milestone in our mission to enable enterprise reinvention in the agentic AI era. By embedding Salesforce-trained engineers directly into our delivery teams, we are moving beyond traditional implementation to true ecosystem orchestration. For our clients, this means we aren’t just ‘setting up’ Agentforce — we are modernizing their digital core to run a unified, agentic workforce at a scale that delivers measurable business value. This production-first model allows us to combine human ingenuity with high-velocity engineering to turn business intent into live, high-performing agents faster than ever before.” — Manish Sharma, Chief Strategy & Services Officer, Accenture “At Deloitte Digital, we believe the agentic front-office creates value when it is trusted, governed, and built for production from the start. The Salesforce FDE Partner Network reflects an important shift from traditional implementation to embedded engineering. It brings together deep technical expertise, rigorous data architecture, and compliance by design, giving the C-suite the confidence to move from pilot to production with greater speed.” — Karl Rupilius, US Salesforce Alliance Lead, Deloitte Consulting LLP This isn’t just about implementation; it’s about how these systems are built from the ground up. Specialized Product Development Outsourcers (PDOs), including Appiphony, Aquiva Labs, and Bridgenext, are bringing these same high engineering standards to the developers creating apps for AgentExchange. This ensures that the solutions customers buy are as robust as the ones they build, with every application engineered for enterprise-grade production from day one. By combining global scale with specialized development expertise, the network provides the unmatched breadth needed to support the most complex business goals in the agentic era. The Methodology: How FDEs Operationalize Success Whether through Salesforce Professional Services or the FDE Partner Network, the delivery model relies on specialized, outcome-based teams that work side by side with customers to drive a sprint from priority use case to a live, compliant agent. An example of an FDE delivery model includes these components: Solution Architecture Design: Before a line of code is written, FDEs define how the agent connects to enterprise data, what permissions govern its actions, and where compliance controls sit. This is aligned to Salesforce best practices from day one, not retrofitted at the end. Agentic Design: A detailed blueprint for how the agent behaves in production. Every decision, action, and escalation path is mapped, with trust guardrails and compliance checks built in before the build phase begins. This eliminates the surprises that derail enterprise deployments when it matters most. Go-Live and Post-Activation Hypercare: FDEs stay engaged after launch to monitor agentic behavior, resolve issues that only surface in production, and fine-tune for sustained performance. Launch day is the starting line, not the finish line. Adoption and Change Management: The most technically perfect agent fails if users don’t trust it. FDEs build and execute adoption plans for readiness, onboarding, and long-term usage. Related Chiesi Group Selects Salesforce Agentforce Life Sciences to Transform Customer Engagement 5 min read How to Build for the Agentic Enterprise | TDX 2026 Keynote Recap The Bottom Line In the agentic era, success is measured by what runs in production and, ultimately, the business value delivered. While every company relies on proprietary data and workflows, the organizations pulling ahead are those with the engineering discipline to wire that uniqueness directly into their agentic workforce. Salesforce provides a proven path from AI investment to lasting outcomes through a unified offering of Success Plans, Professional Services, and now the FDE Partner Network. By combining expert backing with internal technical rigor, Salesforce helps to ensure that every agentic deployment is built for high performance and long-term stability. Find a Salesforce FDE partner on AgentExchange to move from pilot to production and achieve business outcomes at scale as a true Agentic Enterprise. Learn more: Learn how Salesforce Partners transform businesses into Agentic Enterprises See what’s new in AgentExchange Learn how Salesforce is driving customer value through the new Salesforce Partner program Hear how FDEs solve enterprise complexity with Agentforce Read why today’s hottest role is the Forward Deployed Engineer *IDC FutureScape: Worldwide Generative Artificial Intelligence 2025 Predictions, #US52632924, October 2024 Share article Just For You TDX Live Blog: All the Highlights You Missed 22 min read Introducing Salesforce Headless 360. items.title: Why Modern Networks Are Moving DDoS Defense to the Edge (3 minute read)
items.titleZh: 为何现代网络正将DDoS防御迁移至边缘（3分钟阅读）
items.summary:
# Network Security Brief  
*Your weekly roundup of critical network security insights — April 16, 2026*  

---  

**Why Modern Networks Are Shifting DDoS Defense to the Edge**  

Traditional DDoS mitigation is failing today’s threat landscape: 2025 saw attacks double, network-layer threats triple, and 78% of attacks last <5 minutes (per Cisco data). AI botnets like Aisuru and Kimwolf now launch stealthy, 31 Tbps hyper-volumetric attacks—too fast for central scrubbers to handle.  

Cisco’s solution? **Secure DDoS Edge Protection**—a containerized tool deployed directly on Cisco IOS XR routers (no central collectors needed). Key benefits:  
- **Ultra-fast response**: Detect/mitigate in <30 seconds (no backhaul latency).  
- **ML-powered defense**: Establishes host baselines to spot zero-day anomalies.  
- **Multi-use case coverage**:  
  - Inbound peering: Stops L3-L7 floods before reaching core networks.  
  - Outbound access: Blocks botnets using customer CPE as launchpads (prevents peering IP blacklisting).  
  - East-west: Monitors internal traffic to stop horizontal spread.  

**Business wins**:  
- **60% TCO savings**: Ditches dedicated scrubbers and backhaul costs.  
- **MSSP revenue stream**: Supports 10k+ multi-tenant customers with tiered plans, customizable logic, and branded portals.  

This edge-first approach integrates security into existing routing hardware—preparing networks for tomorrow’s DDoS threats without extra overhead.  

---  

*Source: Cisco Blogs (April 16, 2026)*
items.summaryZh:
# 网络安全简报  
*每周关键网络安全洞察汇总 — 2026年4月16日*  

---  

**为何现代网络将DDoS防御转向边缘侧**  

传统DDoS缓解方案已无法应对当前威胁态势：据思科数据，2025年攻击数量翻倍、网络层威胁增至三倍，且78%的攻击持续时间不足5分钟。Aisuru、Kimwolf等AI僵尸网络如今能发起隐蔽的31 Tbps超大规模攻击——速度之快，中央清洗设备难以招架。  

思科的解决方案是什么？**安全DDoS边缘防护**——一款可直接部署在思科IOS XR路由器上的容器化工具（无需中央收集器）。核心优势：  
- **超快速响应**：30秒内完成检测与缓解（无回传延迟）。  
- **机器学习驱动防御**：建立主机基线，识别零日异常。  
- **多场景覆盖**：  
  - 入向对等连接：在流量到达核心网络前阻止L3-L7层攻击。  
  - 出向访问：阻止以客户CPE为跳板的僵尸网络（避免对等IP被拉黑）。  
  - 东西向流量：监控内部流量，阻止横向扩散。  

**业务价值**：  
- **降低60%总拥有成本**：无需专用清洗设备及回传成本。  
- **为MSSP创造收入来源**：支持1万+多租户客户，提供分层套餐、可定制逻辑及品牌化门户。  

这种边缘优先的方式将安全功能整合到现有路由硬件中——无需额外开销，即可让网络应对未来DDoS威胁。  

---  

*来源：思科博客（2026年4月16日）*
items.content: Skip to content Cisco Blogs Executive Platform AI Networking Data Center Security More All Industries All Countries + Regions Cisco Insider Cisco on Cisco CX Developer High Tech Policy Industrial IoT Innovation Learn with Cisco Our Corporate Purpose Partner SP360: Service Provider SMB: Small and Medium Business We Are Cisco Subscribe to Cisco Blogs Menu Search Search Voice Search is currently unavailable Powered by Google Web Speech API We didn't hear that. Try again. When autocomplete results are available use up and down arrows to review and enter to select DownloadsCertificationsDesign GuidesTrainingCommunityCareers Cisco Blogs Cisco Blogs / SP360: Service Provider / Why modern networks are moving DDoS defense to the edge April 16, 2026 Leave a Comment SP360: Service Provider Why modern networks are moving DDoS defense to the edge3 min read Raja Kolagatla In today’s rapidly evolving threat landscape, traditional DDoS mitigation techniques are no longer sufficient for modern service provider networks. In 2025, the number of DDoS attacks nearly doubled and network layer attacks nearly tripled.1 Additionally, 78% of DDoS attacks observed in 2025 lasted five minutes or less,2 making quick detection all the more vital. Hyper-volumetric attacks reached a staggering peak of 31 Tbps in 2025, with AI-driven botnets like Aisuru and Kimwolf infecting millions of devices to launch stealthy, high-impact campaigns.3 Guarding against evolving DDoS attacks: Cisco Secure DDoS Edge Protection Cisco Secure DDoS Edge Protection helps organizations guard against these threats, offering a simplified architecture that utilizes a modular, containerized design and turns your network edge into a distributed security shield. Figure 1: Cisco Secure DDoS Edge Protection solution architecture On-box detection and mitigation Instead of exporting NetFlow data to a central collector, customers can deploy Cisco Secure DDoS Edge Protection containers directly on Cisco IOS XR routers to analyze the traffic samples. Cisco extends the traditional NetFlow to Protobuf, with additional parameters to be captured from the packet headers, which will help enable: Ultra-fast response: Detection and mitigation occur in under 30 seconds. Zero added latency: Because the attacks are mitigated at the edge, there is no backhauling to scrubbing centers and no impact on legitimate traffic performance. The system can also use advanced machine learning (ML) algorithms to establish baselines for every host, effectively identifying behavioral anomalies and neutralizing zero-day threats. Comprehensive use case support Cisco Secure DDoS Edge Protection equips organizations to guard against and respond to a variety of cyberattacks, whether inbound, outbound, or originating from east-west traffic. Peering (inbound) Inbound peering traffic is often the target of hyper-volumetric attacks designed to saturate infrastructure before it can reach a scrubber. Dynamic detection algorithms re-characterize the defense logic based on the attack vectors—in real time as attack vectors change, protecting the core from massive L3–L7 volumetric floods. Access/broadband (outbound) Botnets like Aisuru are infecting the end user customer premises equipment (CPE) to use service provider networks as an “attack launchpad” for DDoS attacks, camouflaging as legitimate traffic. Once the origin of the attack is known, the service provider’s peering IP addresses get blacklisted. As a result, it’s no longer just security operations (SecOps) teams that have to worry about DDoS attacks; network operations (NetOps) teams must also take a more central role in addressing DDoS issues. Cisco Secure DDoS Edge Protection identifies the attacks directly at the access router and mitigates them. East-west traffic Cisco Secure DDoS Edge Protection closes the visibility gaps in the aggregation networks by monitoring internal traffic, preventing malicious flows from spreading horizontally between users and helping service provider networks avoid choking. Compatible with routing platforms Cisco Provider Connectivity routing platforms (ASR 9000, NCS 5500 Series, NCS 5700 Series, NCS 540 Series, 8000 Series) have application hosting capabilities and run the Cisco Secure DDoS Edge Protection agent. These routing platforms empower teams to mitigate attack traffic in a granular manner with attack vectors fed into the user-defined fields of the access lists. Additionally, the platforms also support other traditional mitigation methods of BGP Flowspec-based diversion or rate limiting and BGP Remotely Triggered Black Hole (RTBH). Reduced total cost of ownership (TCO) Cisco Secure DDoS Edge Protection helps save costs across the board, by avoiding dedicated hardware, power, and the hosting of scrubbers; it also eliminates the need for backhaul network capacity to route the traffic to centralized scrubbing centers. Teams enjoy predictable and future-proof costs without needing to add capacity every year. Realistic comparisons indicate potential TCO savings of up to 60% compared to traditional scrubber-based deployments.4 Unlocking new revenue streams: The MSSP opportunity The solution offers built-in support for managed security service providers (MSSPs) included with the license, allowing service providers to turn DDoS protection into a potential revenue stream. Massive multi-tenancy: Onboard 10,000+ customers with full data isolation. Tiered service models: Create tiered plans like Bronze, Silver, and Gold, with different service level agreements (SLAs) and flexible detection and mitigation policies. Customizable logic: Define specific actions tailored to individual customer needs with the built-in scripting language. Customer-facing portals: Provide branded reports and real-time dashboards that show the value of the service during active attacks. Preparing for the next generation of DDoS threats By integrating security directly into Cisco routers, you can reduce TCO, improve customer experience, and make sure your network is ready for the next generation of evolving DDoS threats. Fortify your network edge against hyper-volumetric threats with Cisco Secure DDoS Edge Protection 2025 Q4 DDoS threat report: A record-setting 31.4 Tbps attack caps a year of massive DDoS assaults, Cloudflare, February 5, 2026. DDoS in 2025: what a difference a year makes, TechRadar, January 13, 2026. See note 1. Potential TCO savings based on Cisco calculations for a 4 Tbps peering network, comparing Cisco Secure DDoS Edge Protection to Cisco estimates for a traditional scrubber-based deployment. Additional resources: Cisco IOS XR: A simple, modern, and trustworthy network operating system Cisco Service Provider Security Authors Raja Kolagatla Engineering Product Manager Provider Connectivity Tags: DDoS network edge network security Leave a Comment Cancel replyYou must be logged in to post a comment. Intelligent, simple, and resilient Cisco enables service providers to monetize the delivery of assured services and networking for AI-ready connectivity. items.title: Canva Unveils AI 2.0, Shifts Toward Agentic Workflows (3 minute read)
items.titleZh: Canva发布AI 2.0，转向代理驱动的工作流（3分钟阅读）
items.summary:
# TechForward Brief  
*Enterprise tech insights — April 16, 2026*  

---  

Canva just launched its biggest update since 2013: **Canva AI 2.0**, a conversational, agentic platform built to be teams’ end-to-end work hub.  

### Featured: Canva AI 2.0 Reimagines Visual Workflows  
Unveiled at Canva Create in LA, the update transforms Canva into a single idea-to-execution hub. Key capabilities:  
- **Conversational Design**: Generate editable visuals from natural language prompts.  
- **Agentic Orchestration**: Automate multistep tasks (e.g., multichannel campaigns from one brief).  
- **Object-Based Intelligence**: Edit individual design elements without redoing the whole thing.  
- **Living Memory**: Learns user/brand style over time for personalized outputs.  

New workflow integrations (Slack, Notion, Gmail) pull external data (emails, transcripts) to auto-generate docs/pitches. Canva’s in-house AI models (Proteus, Lucid Origin) are 7x faster and 30x cheaper than frontier alternatives. Users can now import AI artifacts from other tools directly into Canva for editing/collaboration.  

**Launch timeline**: Research preview today → first 1M users soon → broader rollout in weeks.  

---  

*Stay ahead: Subscribe for weekly enterprise tech deep dives.*
items.summaryZh:
TechForward简报  
企业科技洞察 | 2026年4月16日  

Canva刚刚推出自2013年以来最大更新：Canva AI 2.0——一款对话式主动智能平台，旨在成为团队端到端工作中枢。  

### 重点：Canva AI 2.0重塑视觉工作流  
该更新在洛杉矶Canva Create活动中发布，将Canva转变为从创意到执行的一站式中枢。核心功能包括：  
- 对话式设计：通过自然语言提示生成可编辑视觉内容  
- 主动智能编排：自动化多步骤任务（例如，从一份简报生成多渠道营销活动）  
- 基于对象的智能：编辑单个设计元素，无需重做整个作品  
- 动态记忆：随时间学习用户/品牌风格，生成个性化输出  

新增工作流集成（Slack、Notion、Gmail）可提取外部数据（邮件、文字记录），自动生成文档和提案。Canva自研AI模型（Proteus、Lucid Origin）比前沿竞品快7倍、成本低30倍。用户现可直接导入其他工具中的AI成果至Canva，进行编辑与协作。  

**发布时间表**：今日开启研究预览 → 近期面向首批100万用户开放 → 数周内全面推广  

保持领先：订阅每周企业科技深度解读。
items.content: [the voice of enterprise and emerging tech] search OUR NETWORK menu account_circle search Home TechForward Awards Cloud Cloud Native AI Security Infra Blockchain Big Data Emerging Tech Policy Apps Iot Women In Tech theCUBE Research Special Reports Video Exclusives CUBE SPECIAL FEATURES RECENT CUBE EVENTS AWARDS SHARE UPDATED 18:29 EDT / APRIL 16 2026 AI Canva unveils Canva AI 2.0, recasting its platform as an agentic system for work by Duncan Riley SHARE Visual communication platform provider Canva Pty Ltd. today unveiled Canva AI 2.0, an overhaul of its platform that offers a conversational, agentic system aimed at becoming the place where teams start and end their workday. The release, announced at the company’s Canva Create event in Los Angeles, is being pitched as Canva’s biggest shift since its 2013 launch, when it moved design from desktop software into the browser. Canva AI 2.0 has been designed to transform Canva into a conversational, agentic platform where teams can go from idea to execution in one place and to deliver a creative partner across the entire process, from the spark of an idea to the final output. The new offering is built around four capabilities. The first one, Conversational Design, generates fully editable designs from natural language prompts, while the second, Agentic Orchestration, coordinates Canva’s suite of tools to execute multistep goals such as building a multichannel campaign from a single brief. The third capability, Object-Based Intelligence, allows for precise edits to individual elements without regenerating the full design. The fourth, Living Memory, provides persistent context that learns a user’s style and brand preferences over time. Alongside the core platform advancements, Canva also introduced a set of workflow features designed to pull outside work into the editor. They include connectors that link Canva to Slack, Notion, Zoom, Gmail, Google Drive and Google Calendar to allow the assistant to draw on emails, transcripts and calendar entries to generate briefing documents, sales pitches and newsletters. A new scheduling feature runs tasks in the background on a set cadence and web research pulls structured, editable information into designs on demand. Other additions include Brand Intelligence for automatic brand application, Sheets AI for structured spreadsheet generation and Canva Code 2.0, which now supports HTML importing so users can bring AI-generated code into the visual editor without rebuilding it. Powering the new version of Canva AI is the company’s in-house research group, Canva Original Research and Exploration, which the company said now employs more than 100 researchers. Canva claims its proprietary models are up to seven times faster and 30 times cheaper than comparable frontier alternatives. The models include Canva Proteus for style transfer, Canva Lucid Origin for image generation and Canva I2V for image-to-video. Lastly, Canva now allows artifacts generated by outside AI tools to be imported directly into its editor, where they become editable and collaborative through the platform’s drag-and-drop interface and can be published as functional websites. The move is in response to a growing pattern of AI platforms integrating with Canva to give users a path from raw generations to structured, scalable output. Canva AI 2.0 is launching as a research preview today, before initially rolling out to the first 1 million users who discover it on the Canva homepage, with broader availability in the coming weeks. items.title: Cisco patches critical remote code execution vulnerabilities in Identity Services Engine software (2 minute read)
items.titleZh: Cisco修复了身份服务引擎软件中的关键远程代码执行漏洞（阅读时长2分钟）
items.summary:
# Cyber Brief Weekly  
*Your quick hit of critical cybersecurity news — April 20, 2026*  

---  

**Cisco ISE Flaws: Immediate Patching Required**  

Networking giant Cisco issued an urgent advisory (April 15, 2026) for two vulnerabilities in its Identity Services Engine (ISE) and ISE-PIC platforms—tools used to secure enterprise network access.  

- **Critical RCE Flaw (CVE-2026-20147, CVSS 9.9):** Authenticated remote attackers can execute malicious code to gain root access. Single-node deployments also risk complete DoS (locks out unauthenticated users until restored). No exploit chaining needed.  
- **Medium Path Traversal (CVE-2026-20148, CVSS4.9):** Authenticated attackers can read arbitrary sensitive files via improper input validation.  

**No workarounds exist—apply patches immediately:**  
- 3.1 → Patch11; 3.2→Patch10;3.3→Patch11;3.4→Patch6;3.5→Patch3  
- Pre-3.1 versions: Migrate to supported releases  

Discovered by TrendAI’s Jonathan Lein, no public PoCs or active exploits are known yet—but prioritize these patches to avoid future risks.  

**Action:** Update your ISE deployments today.  

---  

Follow for more: [LinkedIn] [Twitter] [Google News]

每周网络安全简报  
2026年4月20日 网络安全要闻速览  

思科ISE漏洞：需立即打补丁  

网络巨头思科于2026年4月15日发布紧急预警通知，通报其身份服务引擎（ISE）及ISE-PIC平台存在的两个漏洞。这两款工具用于保障企业网络访问安全。  

- **高危远程代码执行漏洞（CVE-2026-20147，CVSS评分9.9）**：已认证的远程攻击者可执行恶意代码获取root权限。单节点部署还存在完全拒绝服务（DoS）风险——未认证用户会被锁定，直至系统恢复。无需利用漏洞链。  
- **中危路径遍历漏洞（CVE-2026-20148，CVSS评分4.9）**：已认证攻击者可通过不当输入验证读取任意敏感文件。  

无临时解决方案，需立即应用补丁：  
- 3.1版本→补丁11；3.2→补丁10；3.3→补丁11；3.4→补丁6；3.5→补丁3  
- 3.1之前的版本：迁移至受支持的版本  

该漏洞由趋势AI（TrendAI）的Jonathan Lein发现，目前尚无公开的漏洞利用证明（PoC）或活跃攻击利用，但需优先安装这些补丁以规避未来风险。  

行动建议：今日内更新你的ISE部署。  

关注获取更多：[领英] [推特] [谷歌新闻]
items.content: gbhackers. Monday, April 20, 2026 LinkedinRSSTwitter gbhackers. Home Threats Cyber Attack Data Breach Vulnerability What is DFIR Top 10 Search Follow us On Linkedin CVE/vulnerabilityCyber Security NewsVulnerability 2 min.Read Critical Cisco ISE Flaws Let Remote Attackers Execute Malicious Code By Divya April 16, 2026 Share FacebookTwitterPinterestWhatsApp Networking giant Cisco has issued an urgent security advisory warning of two newly discovered vulnerabilities impacting its Identity Services Engine (ISE) and ISE Passive Identity Connector (ISE-PIC). Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE) is a widely deployed security policy management platform that provides secure access to enterprise network resources. The most severe of these new flaws could allow an authenticated, remote attacker to achieve full remote code execution (RCE) and compromise affected infrastructure. According to the official Cisco advisory published on April 15, 2026, the vulnerabilities reside in the web-based management interfaces of both products. While exploitation requires valid administrative credentials, the potential impact remains catastrophic, prompting a critical severity rating. Technical Details and Vulnerability Breakdown The security bulletin outlines two distinct security flaws that operate independently of one another. Threat actors do not need to chain these vulnerabilities together to launch a successful attack. CVE-2026-20147 (CVSS Score 9.9 – Critical): This remote code execution vulnerability stems from insufficient validation of user-supplied input. Threat actors can exploit this by sending maliciously crafted HTTP requests to an affected device. A successful attack grants user-level access to the underlying operating system, which can then be trivially elevated to root privileges. Furthermore, in single-node ISE deployments, this exploit can trigger a complete denial of service (DoS), locking unauthenticated endpoints out of the network until the node is fully restored. CVE-2026-20148 (CVSS Score 4.9 – Medium): This path traversal flaw is caused by improper input validation. By sending specific HTTP requests, authenticated attackers can bypass directory restrictions to read arbitrary, sensitive files stored on the underlying operating system. Mitigation Strategies and Patch Availability Cisco has confirmed that there are currently no available workarounds or temporary mitigations to prevent exploitation. Organizations must apply the official software updates immediately to secure their network environments. Administrators are strongly advised to upgrade their systems according to the following official patch guidance: Deployments running version 3.1 must upgrade to 3.1 Patch 11. Environments using version 3.2 should apply 3.2 Patch 10. Systems operating on version 3.3 are advised to install 3.3 Patch 11. Administrators on version 3.4 need to update to 3.4 Patch 6. Networks utilizing version 3.5 must be updated to 3.5 Patch 3. Legacy versions prior to 3.1 require an immediate migration to a supported, fixed release. These critical vulnerabilities were independently discovered and reported to Cisco by security researcher Jonathan Lein of TrendAI Research. At the time of publication, the Cisco Product Security Incident Response Team (PSIRT) stated they are not aware of any public proof-of-concept (PoC) exploits or active malicious exploitation in the wild. However, given the massive 9.9 CVSS score of CVE-2026-20147, cybersecurity teams are strongly urged to prioritize these patches in their vulnerability management cycles before threat actors can reverse-engineer the software fixes. Follow us on Google News, LinkedIn, and X to Get Instant Updates and Set GBH as a Preferred Source in Google.
